To pull all the data in each row for a client for their MIN(service_date) and their MAX(service_date), you can use the following query: ``` SELECT client_id, MIN(service_date) OVER (PARTITION BY client_id) AS min_service_date, MAX(service_date) OVER (PARTITION BY client_id) AS max_service_date, other_stuff FROM your_table_name ``` This query uses the `OVER` clause to partition the `service_date` column by `client_id`, and the `MIN` and `MAX` functions to retrieve the minimum and maximum `service_date` values for each `client_id` respectively. The `MIN` and `MAX` functions are applied to the `service_date` column for each `client_id` and the results are then selected along with the `client_id` and `other_stuff` columns. texts.full_text: yards 10,643 Average 4.1 Receiving yards 4,248 Receiving Average 9.1 Touchdowns 78 Stats at NFL.com Richard "Ricky" James Watters (born April 7, 1969) is a former football running back who played for the San Francisco 49ers, Philadelphia Eagles, and the Seattle Seahawks of the National Football League. Watters played college football at the University of Notre Dame where he played wide receiver on the school's 1988 champion team. He also won a Super Bowl as a member of the 1994 San Francisco 49ers. Watters was known throughout his playing career for his outstanding receiving skills and his unique high-step running style, which earned him the nickname Ricky "Running" Watters, from ESPN sportcaster Chris Berman. Currently Watters is a motivational speaker for kids who, like himself, are adopted. He is also a recording artist, music producer, President and CEO of Tigero Entertainment and an author, having written the book, For Who For What, a Warrior’s Journey. He was the recent head football coach for Oak Ridge High School, Orlando, Florida until resigning on October 1, 2013. texts.full_text: write a symbolic expression in terms of a specific subexpression. Consider the following scenario: expression f with 2 variables a, b subexpression c = a / b syms a b c f = b / (a + b) % = 1 / (1 + a/b) = 1 / (1 + c) <- what I need Is there a way to achieve this? Edit: The step from 1 / (1 + a/b) to 1 / (1 + c) can be achieved by calling subs(1 / (1 + a/b),a/b,c) So a better formulated question is: Is there a wayYes. You are already on the right track with your provided solution using `subs`. This function replaces one expression or variable in terms of another and seems to be exactly what you need for this task! In fact, it looks like MATLAB's Symbolic Math Toolbox has multiple functions that can help rewrite expressions: 1. rewrite(): Rearranges the given expression according to specified rules/targets (e.g., rewriting trigonometric or logarithmic terms). The toolbox also includes several specialized rewriters for specific tasks such as expanding and factorizing, which I think could be relevant in some cases

Also set to appear in at least one installment is Evan Peters ("Invasion") though details on his role were not given; both episodes have been written by series co-creator Ryan Murphy. (8/26) THE NEIGHBORHOOD WATCH PROJECT (Fox Searchlight): The project, which had previously attached Ben Stiller to star and Akiva Schaffer set as director from a script penned by Seth Rogen & Evan Goldberg has now added another key player: Ruben Fleischer ("Zombieland") is in negotiations for the film's helm. No word on whether or not this development means that either of its previous directors are still involved with "Watch." (8/26) THE OFFICE (NBC): The comedy series will reportedly introduce a new character during season eight: Melora Hardin ("Trancers" franchise, "The Amati Girls") is set to appear in at least 10 episodes as Jill, described only by sources close the production. She'll first be seen on episode two of this upcoming campaign which kicks off September 23 (8/26) THE VAMPIRE DIARIES & THE SECRET CIRCLE: The CW has released a pair of trailers for its returning hit "Vampire" as well and new entry, the Kevin Williamson-produced drama series adaptation from L.J Smith's novels

texts.full_text: t his consent causing Starkey to fire Schwartz. Both players have a wide disdain for each other, and are made to use custom bowls for the game. After failing with his woods, and the pressure oF the game getting to him, Starkey throws his own woods (and signature carrier bag) into a canal. With both players failing at half time, Trevor and Kerry talk to the pair, and get them to work as a team to make a comeback. Starkey would dive into the canal, followed by Ray Speight, who thought Starkey was trying to drown himself, but was in fact rescuing his own bowls. The two return to the arena, wet and make a comeback thanks to Speight's experience and Starkey's extravagance, to force the game into an extra name (humorously titled the "golden bowl"), much to the disbelief of Carl and Mark Doohan. The final end, a one bowl play-off, sees a perfect front toucher from Mark Doohan. Speight gives the bowl to Starkey, but stops him from playing his intended fire shot, and instead instructs him to play his own signature large inswinging bowl. The shot works, and the two celebrate having defeated the Australian team, and make amends with Trevor and Mutley. Specifically, levels of anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory oxylipins derived from linolenic acid (ALA) found in flaxseed, including 13-hydroxyoctadecadienoic acid (13-HODA), 9-hydroxyoctadecadienoic acid (9-HODA), and their epoxy metabolites, were significantly increased. These changes correlated with the reduction in central blood pressure but not with cardiac performance metrics. In summary, dietary flaxseed consumption can reduce central aortic blood pressure through mechanisms involving alterations in plasma oxylipin profiles, particularly those derived from ALA, without significant effects on arterial stiffness or direct cardiac involvement. texts.full_text: tase. Monte Carlo simulations using free energy perturbation theory have been used to guide the selection of bicyclic heterocycles in the lead optimization of non-nucleoside inhibitors (NNIs) of human immunodeficiency virus type 1. The first step was a series of calculations on model compounds that were designed to mimic each component of an NN I, including backbone amides and side-chain aliphatic groups as well as heterocyclic rings with different degrees of substitution at various positions in the ring. This led us to conclude that bicyclic systems containing nitrogen or sulfur would be most likely to succeed if they had a five-membered N-containing ring fused to an aromatic six- (or seven-)membered system, and that there was little difference between pyridines , thiazoles, imidazoles, isothiazolones, benzimidazolidinone s or tetrahydroisoquinolin e derivatives. The second step involved the use of these bicyclic systems in place of a phenyl ring as part of an NN I model compound and testing their influence on binding to HIV-1 reverse transcriptase (RT). A series of compounds was synthesized, each with one such fused heterocycle , and tested for inhibition against wild-type RT. This led us to conclude that the 2-(thiazol-4-yl)phenyl system is optimal in terms of potency when substituted at C(3), whereas a benzimidazolidinone ring would be best if it were attached through an ethylene bridge . [1] 1. FEP-guided selection of bicyclic heterocycles in lead optimization for non-nucleoside inhibitors of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase. Schramm, V.L., Lengauer, T. J. Med. Chem. (1999) [ Pubmed ]

texts.full_text: its 17 reactors by 2022 and is building interim storage facilities for the waste they produce until then. But what will happen when these short-term repositories are full? At present, no one knows where all this radioactive material will go in the long term. Germany’s nuclear industry had hoped that reprocessing plants would be built to separate out reusable uranium from the waste and turn it into fuel for new reactors. But those plans have been abandoned. Now, says Jürgen Möllemann of Greenpeace, a German environmental group: “We need an interim storage facility that is safe in the long term.” The situation at Asse has added to public anxiety about nuclear waste disposal and may make it harder for Germany’s government to persuade people to accept new short-term repositories. Meanwhile, engineers are racing against time to deal with the contamination of groundwater around the salt mine by radioactive waste that has seeped through cracks in the rock. So far they have dug up 2000 drums but there is no end in sight. The whole operation could take years and cost hundreds of millions of euros. texts.full_text: Deadwood Peak takes its name from Deadwood Lakes and Deadwood Creek to its northwest, and their names came from the large number of downed trees in the area. From Chinook Pass, a short scramble up the south side leads to the summit with unobstructed views of Mount Rainier. Climate Deadwood Peak is located in the marine west coast climate zone of western North America. Most weather fronts originate in the Pacific Ocean, and travel northeast toward the Cascade Mountains. As fronts approach the mountains, they are forced to rise, cool, and condense, resulting in precipitation. As a result, the area around Deadwood Peak receives significant rainfall and snowfall throughout the year, with the majority of it falling between October and March. the peak's proximity to Mount Rainier also creates an orographic effect, where the mountain forces warm, moist air to rise and cool, leading to increased precipitation in the area. Empire was shown on ITV in the United Kingdom as Big G. Cast Egan starred in the series at the age of forty-one, having previously been in the hit film A Summer Place. Redigo was a rare ranch manager, having a Master of Business Administration degree. The ranch was located somewhere in the American Southwest, but the exact location was never pinpointed. The Garretts did have an empire. Besides ranching they were involved in oil, agriculture, and mining. The series has unusually- titled episodes. Empire also featured 22-year-old Ryan O'Neal, some two years before he gained greater recognition as Rodney Harrington in ABC's Peyton Place. texts.full_text: 55 to 75 years showed that current smokers and ex-smokers had significant 1.96-fold and 1.86-fold risks of having more than 8 missing teeth, respectively.^[@r16]^ That study also showed that ex-smokers who had smoked for 21 years or longer had a significantly higher risk of having more than 8 missing teeth, as compared with never smokers, and that the risk of having more than 8 missing teeth among ex-smokers who had stopped smoking for more than 21 years was equal to that of never smokers.^[@r16]^ The present study showed that, as compared with never smokers, male ex-smokers aged 65 to 79 years, but not those aged 40 to 64 years, had a significantly higher risk of having 19 or fewer teeth. Mean number of smoking years was higher in men aged 65 to 79 years (29.3 years) than in men aged 40 to 64 years (20.8 years). The longer duration of smoking among elderly men may increase the risk of having 19 or fewer teeth among ex-smokers. In this study, low education level was significantly associated with tooth loss among men aged 40 to 64 years and men aged 65 to 79 years. This finding is consistent with the results of previous studies in various populations.^[@r07],[@r12]--[@r15]^ Low education level was found to be associated with less utilization of dental care^[@r28]^ and unfavorable oral health-related behaviors,^[@r29]^ which could lead to tooth loss

texts.full_text: and tab pages have different purposes so preferring one to the other makes no sense whatsoever. The issue with buffers and tabs is one of confusion, caused by a combination of independent facts. Most "modern" text editors and IDEs use a tab metaphor to represent loaded files. That metaphor acts as an information system — it shows the user what files are opened and their state — and as an intAs ZyX said on #vim, this question sounds like 'Why do Vim experts prefer tasty over warm?'. "Vim expert" don't necessarily have a preference for buffers or tabs; they use them based upon the task at hand. Buffers are used to hold file contents and can be accessed from multiple places in an editor while tab pages act as workspace dividers that organize different parts of your work. The confusion between using buffer versus tag often stems due primarily because both concepts have been conflated with a single interface feature (the :ls command). However, they serve very distinct purposes. Buffers are essentially Vim's way to manage multiple files simultaneously without the need for them all being loaded into memory at once; it provides an efficient method of storing and retrieving data across different tab pages or windows. texts.full_text: renal-transplant recipients^[@CR6],[@CR7]^. The cause of increasing incidence of BKVN is still unknown^[@CR7]^. At present, excessive immunotherapy, e.g. tacrolimus and mycophenalate mofetil, might be the primary risk factors of BKVN^[@CR8]--[@CR10]^. The common therapy for BKV nephropathy (BKVN) involves reducing or adjusting antirejection medication to allow immune reconstitution while monitoring viral load and kidney function. This approach is often referred as "immune restoration" strategy, which aims at balancing the suppression of BKV replication with maintaining adequate immunosuppression for graft acceptance. 1. Reduction/Withdrawal Therapy: The primary step in managing active or impending BKVN involves reducing doses—or even temporarily withdrawing—immunosupppressive drugs like tacrolimus and mycophenolate mofetil, which are known to contribute significantly toward viral reactivation^[@CR8]--[^@cr10]. This reduction helps the immune system recover its ability to control virus replication. 2. Cidofovir Administration: Cidovir is an antiviral agent that has been used in treating BKVN due to direct inhibition of polyomavirus DNA polymerase, thus reducing viral load^[@CR11]^. However, it must be administered with caution as nephrotoxicity can occur. 3.
